summary: Added a Chapter Runway sequence rail to the Path Map Chapter Archive so the active chapter lens becomes a playable visual chain with progress, glyph stages, reveal-more depth, and direct Event Proof plus Route Replay synchronization.

queries: UX timeline drilldown event sequence filtering dashboard pattern milestones blockers
game UI progression timeline level unlock path design pattern
activity timeline event log drill down UX design pattern
pattern_notes: Timeline patterns make event history easier to scan when each point stays clickable and chronological.
Game progression systems work by making the journey, current position, and next reward visible.
Faceted filters and log drilldowns reduce overload when long histories need to stay expandable without flattening into one giant list.

ui_contract: Render a Chapter Runway inside the selected Chapter Archive record deck.
Use the active chapter lens as the runway input, so All, Proof, Unlock, Service, Task, Trace, and Gate lenses each get their own sequence rail.
Show twelve runway stages by default, using existing event glyphs and passed/active states.
Reveal more runway stages in browser-local batches without mutating snapshot data.
Clicking a runway node focuses Event Proof and synchronizes Route Replay through the existing event-focus contract.
Preserve responsive wrapping with no horizontal overflow or clipped runway labels.

red_green.red: pytest tests\test_agent_company_atlas_path_chapter_runway.py -q failed before implementation on missing pathChapterRunwayLimitByLane.

browser_green_route.evidence: Rendered Chapter Runway with 12 nodes, header 'Chapter Runway Full event chain 1 / 43', reveal 'Extend runway by 12', no bad rectangles, and no horizontal overflow.

lens_and_reveal.evidence: Selecting Proof changed the runway to 'proof chain 1 / 22' with 12 proof nodes and reveal 'Extend runway by 10'. Revealing expanded the proof runway to 22 nodes and removed the reveal control.

focus_sync.evidence: Clicking the second proof runway node focused 'Digital products local post-approval simulation plan', moved Event Proof to 4 / 64, and kept Route Replay synchronized to the latest matching replay stage.

active_fallback.evidence: Fresh route load shows one visible active runway node even when browser-local event focus points outside the visible first batch.

responsive.evidence: All tested viewports rendered one runway, twelve nodes, one active node, no bad rectangles, no clipped runway text, and no horizontal overflow.
